code:
def is_sparse(n: int) -> bool:
    """
    Checks if a number is sparse. A number is sparse if there are no consecutive 1s 
    in its binary representation.

    Args:
        n: The integer to check.

    Returns:
        True if the number is sparse, False otherwise.
    """
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("Input must be a non-negative integer.")
    
    # A number is sparse if n & (n >> 1) is 0
    # n >> 1 shifts all bits of n one position to the right.
    # If there are two consecutive 1s in n, say at positions k and k+1,
    # then after shifting, the 1 at position k+1 will move to k.
    # The original 1 at position k will still be there.
    # So, performing a bitwise AND operation will result in a 1 at position k.
    # If there are no consecutive 1s, the AND operation will result in 0.
    return (n & (n >> 1)) == 0
